name: Welcome Sequence (4-Part)
description: Four-email onboarding series: Welcome, Story Behind the Author, World of Your Books, and The Invitation. Open rates exceed 50%. The cornerstone of every author email program.
goalExit: Reader clicks a book link or makes a first purchase

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: New subscriber joins list

steps.label: Email 1 — Welcome
steps.detail: Introduce yourself, set expectations, deliver lead magnet if promised. Goal: strong first impression, establish author brand.

steps.label: Wait 1–2 Days
steps.detail: Delay: 1–2 days

steps.label: Email 2 — Story Behind the Author
steps.detail: Your background, writing journey, and passion. Personal and memorable. Goal: emotional connection beyond the books.

steps.label: Wait 2–3 Days
steps.detail: Delay: 2–3 days

steps.label: Email 3 — World of Your Books
steps.detail: Introduce catalog, highlight best entry-point title. Soft sales moment, not a hard pitch. Goal: guide toward first purchase.

steps.label: Wait 3–5 Days
steps.detail: Delay: 3–5 days

steps.label: Email 4 — The Invitation
steps.detail: Reader group, key platform, or what to expect. Direct CTA toward most important title. Goal: complete onboarding, convert warm subscribers.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader clicks a book link

name: Reader Magnet Delivery
description: Delivers promised free content instantly on sign-up. Two emails + conditional branch: delivery email fires in seconds, 24–48hr check detects non-downloaders, follow-up removes barriers. Connects to Welcome Sequence on completion.
goalExit: Free content delivered and reader clicks the download link

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: New subscriber opts in via reader magnet form — fires within seconds of form submission

steps.label: Delivery Email
steps.detail: Lead with download link first. Brief warm introduction (2–3 sentences). Forward frame toward welcome sequence. Subject: "Your copy of [Title] is here"

steps.label: Wait 24–48 Hours
steps.detail: ScribeCount checks whether reader clicked the download link during this window

steps.label: Downloaded?
steps.detail: YES → tag confirmed-download, move to Welcome Sequence. NO → send follow-up email.

steps.label: Delivery Follow-Up
steps.detail: Warm, helpful tone. Resend link prominently. Acknowledge possible spam filter. Invite reply for support. Subject: "Did your copy of [Title] arrive okay?"

steps.label: Wait 1–2 Days
steps.detail: Move to Welcome Sequence regardless of download status

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader clicks download link — tagged confirmed-download, transitions to Welcome Sequence

name: Order Confirmation & Digital Delivery
description: Immediate official receipt then digital content delivery. Absence of this email creates anxiety and support requests. Goal: instant reassurance, reduce support inquiries.
goalExit: Digital content delivered and confirmed opened

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Completed order in direct store

steps.label: Order Confirmation
steps.detail: Summarize order, amount charged, delivery details. Warm on-brand message, not a cold generic receipt.

steps.label: Digital Delivery
steps.detail: Clear prominent download link. Simple instructions. Note on compatible devices/apps.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader opens delivery email

name: Post-Purchase Thank You (First Purchase)
description: Sent immediately after first purchase. Confirms, appreciates, suggests related title or review opportunity. Reduces buyer remorse and opens door to second sale.
goalExit: Reader clicks a catalog or series link

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: First-time purchase completed

steps.label: First Purchase Thank You
steps.detail: Confirm purchase, express genuine appreciation. Suggest related title, invite reader group, or point toward review opportunity while excitement is fresh.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader clicks catalog or series link

name: Repeat Purchase Thank You
description: Triggered on second or subsequent purchase. Warmer and more personal than first thank you. Acknowledges loyalty, offers exclusive perk. Deepens relationship and increases lifetime reader value.
goalExit: Reader accepts exclusive perk or joins reader community

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Reader completes second or subsequent purchase

steps.label: Loyalty Thank You
steps.detail: Acknowledge loyalty, make them feel recognized. Offer exclusive perk: early access, bonus, or reader community invitation.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader accepts perk or joins community

name: Post-Purchase Follow-up
description: Sent 3–5 days after purchase when reading experience is fresh and emotion is high. Invites review, asks for feedback, or suggests next book while enthusiasm is at its peak.
goalExit: Reader leaves a review or clicks next book link

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: 3–5 days after digital delivery confirmed opened

steps.label: Follow-up Email
steps.detail: Invite honest review, ask for feedback, or suggest next book in series while enthusiasm is at its peak.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader leaves review or clicks next book

name: Abandoned Cart
description: First email fires 1 hour after abandonment — while the book is still in the reader's mental foreground. Two-email sequence. Conversion rate 5–15%. Lead with the book, not the cart. Include cover image and social proof.
goalExit: Reader completes the purchase

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Reader adds book to cart and leaves without progressing to checkout

steps.label: Wait 1 Hour
steps.detail: Delay: 1 hour (configurable) — confirms real abandonment without letting interest cool

steps.label: Already Purchased?
steps.detail: If purchase event received — goal exit; else — send reminder

steps.label: Cart Reminder — Email 1
steps.detail: Lead with the book. Cover image. Brief hook. One line of reader praise. One button back to product page or pre-populated cart.

steps.label: Wait 24 Hours
steps.detail: Delay: 24 hours

steps.label: Purchased?
steps.detail: If purchased — goal exit; else — final nudge

steps.label: Final Nudge — Email 2
steps.detail: Two or three sentences. Light reminder, no urgency language. After two emails, flow ends.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader completes purchase — post-purchase sequence begins

name: Abandoned Checkout
description: First email fires in 30 minutes — faster than cart because intent is higher. Reader entered payment info. Two-email sequence. Direct acknowledgment, friction removal, cart restoration link.
goalExit: Reader completes checkout

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Reader reaches checkout, enters payment or shipping info, but does not complete

steps.label: Wait 30 Minutes
steps.detail: Delay: 30 minutes (configurable) — higher intent warrants faster response

steps.label: Already Purchased?
steps.detail: If purchase event received — goal exit; else — send reminder

steps.label: Checkout Reminder — Email 1
steps.detail: Direct acknowledgment. Address technical friction: payment methods, guest checkout, money-back guarantee. "Hit reply and I'll sort it out personally." Cart restoration link if platform supports it.

steps.label: Wait 24 Hours
steps.detail: Delay: 24 hours

steps.label: Purchased?
steps.detail: If purchased — goal exit; else — 24-hour follow-up

steps.label: 24-Hour Follow-Up — Email 2
steps.detail: Shorter, warmer. Invitation to reach out if anything went wrong. After two emails, flow ends.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader completes purchase — post-purchase sequence begins

name: Review Request
description: A focused, standalone automation dedicated entirely to generating social proof. Fires 4–7 days after purchase as a single clear ask. More effective than burying the review request inside a longer email with multiple purposes.
goalExit: Reader clicks a review link on any platform

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: 4–7 days after completed purchase

steps.label: Already Left Review?
steps.detail: If reader clicked review link in Post-Purchase Follow-Up — goal exit; else — continue

steps.label: Review Request
steps.detail: Single focused ask with direct links to review submission pages on each platform. Personal framing, genuine curiosity about reader's experience.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader clicks any review link — no further review requests sent for this title

name: Refund Confirmation
description: Handles refunds with professionalism and grace. Opportunity to leave lasting positive impression and keep door open for return.
goalExit: Refund processed and reader acknowledged

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Refund request processed in direct store

steps.label: Refund Confirmation
steps.detail: Confirm refund clearly, provide timeline for funds return. Invite feedback, leave door open with genuine low-pressure closing.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Refund acknowledged — reader remains on list

name: Preorder Confirmation & Nurture
description: Three-phase flow anchored to release date. Confirmation honors commitment immediately. Nurture sequence (4 emails) keeps enthusiasm alive. Fulfillment delivers the payoff. Preorder readers suppressed from launch broadcast.
goalExit: Fulfillment email opened and download link clicked

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Preorder completed — reader tagged as preorder-reader immediately

steps.label: Preorder Confirmation
steps.detail: Confirm transaction and release date. Clarify billing timing. Acknowledge early commitment. Brief tease. "Preorder readers hear from me first."

steps.label: Release-Date Anchor
steps.detail: All nurture emails scheduled relative to release date. Updating release date reschedules everything automatically.

steps.label: Behind the Scenes (7 weeks before)
steps.detail: Exclusive personal note about writing the book. The anecdote you haven't shared anywhere else.

steps.label: Excerpt (4 weeks before)
steps.detail: First chapter or compelling excerpt. Reward and commitment-reinforcement.

steps.label: Cover Story (2 weeks before)
steps.detail: The story behind the cover — design process, the detail most readers won't notice.

steps.label: Countdown (1 week before)
steps.detail: Brief and energetic. Release date now imminent. The drumroll before the payoff.

steps.label: Preorder Fulfillment (release day)
steps.detail: Celebration first. Download link prominently placed. Acknowledge reader's role. Plant review seed.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Download link clicked — reader transitions to post-purchase flow

name: Series Completion
description: Four routing scenarios based on catalog position and purchase history. Catches readers at the moment of highest emotional readiness. One of the most reliably profitable automations in the flow library.
goalExit: Reader purchases the recommended next title

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Reader purchases final book in a series — identified via AuthorVault catalog metadata

steps.label: Wait 3–5 Days
steps.detail: Configurable. Long enough to finish reading, short enough to catch emotional momentum.

steps.label: Catalog Position Check
steps.detail: Routes to one of four scenarios based on purchase history and AuthorVault catalog structure

steps.label: Scenario 1 — Next Series
steps.detail: Acknowledge milestone. Single targeted recommendation with connection explanation. One CTA.

steps.label: Scenario 2 — Standalone
steps.detail: Lead with what's most similar. Position as different kind of excellence, not lesser commitment.

steps.label: Scenario 3 — Mid-Series
steps.detail: Warm direct recommendation for next book in sequence. Brief hook without spoilers.

steps.label: Scenario 4 — Catalog Complete
steps.detail: Relationship deepening. What's coming, community invite, exclusive content.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader purchases recommended next title

name: Review Request
description: Asks for social proof at the right moment — when reading experience is fresh, not immediately upon purchase.
goalExit: Reader clicks a review platform link

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: 5 days after digital delivery confirmed opened

steps.label: Review Ask
steps.detail: Invite honest review while enthusiasm is at its peak.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader clicks a review platform link

name: ARC Invitation
description: Invites most engaged readers to receive a free early copy in exchange for an honest review at launch. Builds launch-day review base before book goes live.
goalExit: Reader accepts ARC invitation and is tagged arc-reader

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Sent to most engaged subscribers segment

steps.label: ARC Invitation
steps.detail: Explain what an ARC is. Outline simple expectations: timing and where to post review. Convey exclusive insider nature so readers feel genuinely selected, not mass-solicited.

steps.label: Accepted?
steps.detail: If clicked accept — tag as arc-reader; else — no action

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader tagged as arc-reader

name: ARC Confirmation & Follow-up
description: Delivers ARC copy, checks in during reading, then follows up near release to maximize review conversion. Goal: maximize review conversion, ensure advance reader program translates into launch-day impact.
goalExit: Reader posts review on launch day

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Tag added: arc-reader

steps.label: ARC Delivery
steps.detail: Deliver ARC immediately and reliably.

steps.label: Wait 3 Days
steps.detail: Delay: 3 days

steps.label: Check-In Email
steps.detail: Warm check-in, no pressure.

steps.label: Wait Until Near Release
steps.detail: Wait until 3–5 days before release date

steps.label: ARC Follow-up
steps.detail: Gentle reminder to post review. Warm and appreciative, not pressuring. Include direct links to review pages on key platforms.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader posts review — launch-day social proof secured

name: Book Launch
description: High-energy broadcast on or just before release day. Creates urgency and excitement. Generates concentrated burst of sales in the critical launch window to signal momentum to retail algorithms.
goalExit: Reader purchases or pre-orders on launch day

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Scheduled broadcast — sent on or just before release day

steps.label: Launch Announcement
steps.detail: Share cover, compelling hook from blurb, early reader praise, clear CTA to purchase or pre-order link. Deliberate high-energy moment designed to create urgency and excitement.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader purchases or pre-orders

name: New Release Notification
description: Targets existing readers who already know and trust your work — warmer and more direct than a launch email. Connects new release to titles they have already enjoyed.
goalExit: Reader purchases the new title

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: New title published — sent to backlist readers and engaged subscribers segment

steps.label: New Release Notification
steps.detail: Connect new release to titles they have already enjoyed. Highlight what makes this book fresh while feeling familiar. Clear immediate path to purchase.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader purchases new title

name: Re-engagement
description: Catches inactive subscribers at 60, 90, or 180 days and gives them a genuine reason to reconnect before clean removal. A smaller engaged list always outperforms a large unresponsive one.
goalExit: Reader opens or clicks — re-engagement confirmed

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: No open in 90 days

steps.label: Miss You Email
steps.detail: Low-pressure check-in. Remind them why they subscribed, share what they have missed, or offer compelling incentive.

steps.label: Wait 7 Days
steps.detail: Delay: 7 days

steps.label: Opened?
steps.detail: If opened — re-engaged; if not — final email

steps.label: Last Chance Email
steps.detail: Final notice before removal from active list.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader opens or clicks — stays on list

name: Milestone Celebration
description: Relationship-forward automation for anniversaries, birthdays, and catalog milestones. Reader feels seen, not marketed to.
goalExit: Milestone email opened and reader engages

steps.label: Anniversary Trigger
steps.detail: Join date anniversary — automatic from subscriber record

steps.label: Anniversary Email
steps.detail: Recognition first, optional gift second

steps.label: Birthday Trigger
steps.detail: Birth month/day in profile — on birth date

steps.label: Birthday Email
steps.detail: Brief, warm, genuinely celebratory

steps.label: Catalog Trigger
steps.detail: Purchase count threshold via store webhook

steps.label: Catalog Milestone Email
steps.detail: Acknowledge purchase milestone, optional community invite

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Opened and engaged — loyalty deepened

name: Giveaway Announcement
description: Broadcast introducing a contest or giveaway. Leads with the prize, explains how to enter, communicates deadline. Turns existing list into a growth engine for new readers.
goalExit: Subscribers enter giveaway and share with friends

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Giveaway launched — sent as broadcast

steps.label: Giveaway Announcement
steps.detail: Lead with the prize. Clearly explain how to enter. Communicate deadline to create urgency. Encourage sharing with friends.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Subscribers enter and share

name: Newsletter Swap / Cross-Promotion
description: Introduces a fellow author as part of a mutual cross-promotion. Feels like a trusted personal recommendation, not an advertisement.
goalExit: Subscribers click through to recommended author

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Newsletter swap or cross-promotion scheduled — sent as broadcast

steps.label: Cross-Promotion Email
steps.detail: Feel like a trusted personal recommendation. Lead with why your readers specifically will enjoy this author.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Subscribers click through to recommended author

name: Limited Time Offer / Flash Sale
description: Short-window price reduction. Lead with offer and deadline immediately — urgency is the engine. Generates concentrated spike in sales activity.
goalExit: Reader purchases during sale window

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Flash sale activated — sent as broadcast, typically 24 hours to a few days

steps.label: Flash Sale Email
steps.detail: Lead with offer and deadline immediately. Combine discounted price with strong reminder of book value so deal feels like opportunity, not clearance.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader purchases during sale window

name: Price Drop Notification
description: Calmer than a flash sale — less about urgency, more about removing a barrier. Re-surfaces a title to readers who may have passed on it at full price.
goalExit: Price-sensitive reader converts to buyer

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Price permanently or temporarily reduced — automated or manually triggered

steps.label: Price Drop Email
steps.detail: Re-surface title to readers who may have passed on it at full price. Pair price announcement with fresh look at book hook, reviews, or reader praise.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader purchases at new price

name: Box Set / Bundle Announcement
description: Notifies subscribers that multiple titles are now available as a single discounted package. Leads with value proposition immediately. Increases average order value.
goalExit: Reader purchases the bundle

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Box set or bundle published — sent as broadcast

steps.label: Bundle Announcement
steps.detail: Lead with value proposition immediately: how many books, how much savings, why this collection belongs together. Make deal feel curated not convenient.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader purchases the bundle

name: Survey / Feedback Request
description: Invites subscribers to share opinions on books, emails, or reader experience. Brief and respectful of their time. Gathers actionable intelligence.
goalExit: Reader completes survey or replies with feedback

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Broadcast or automated — sent to subscriber list or targeted segment

steps.label: Survey Request
steps.detail: Brief and respectful of their time. Lead with why their specific input matters. Keep ask focused and survey short.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader completes survey or replies

name: Reader Group / Community Invitation
description: Invites subscribers to join a dedicated reader space. Moves loyal readers from passive email recipients into an active connected community.
goalExit: Reader joins the community

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Broadcast — target most engaged subscribers first

steps.label: Community Invitation
steps.detail: Paint vivid picture of what life inside the community looks like. Make invitation feel like opportunity not obligation. Give sense of founding-member status.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader joins the community

name: Event Announcement
description: Notifies subscribers of an upcoming appearance, live session, or experience. Brings reader community together around a shared live experience.
goalExit: Reader registers or RSVPs for the event

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Event scheduled — sent as broadcast with follow-up reminder sequence

steps.label: Event Announcement
steps.detail: Lead with what, when, and where in first sentence. Clear registration or RSVP link that removes every barrier between interest and attendance.

steps.label: Wait Until 2 Days Before
steps.detail: Wait until 2 days before event

steps.label: Event Reminder
steps.detail: Brief reminder with registration link. Build final anticipation.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Reader registers or RSVPs

name: Unsubscribe — Reader's Choice
description: Fires when a subscriber actively opts out. Mandatory: confirm immediately and unambiguously. Optional: offer preference alternative, acknowledge relationship gracefully, leave door open. Protects deliverability — a clean unsubscribe is always better than a spam complaint.
goalExit: Unsubscribe confirmed cleanly — goodwill preserved, re-subscribe path provided

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Reader clicks unsubscribe link and confirms opt-out

steps.label: Unsubscribe Confirmation
steps.detail: Subject: 'You have been unsubscribed' or 'You are off the list' or 'All done — you have been removed' — First sentence: 'You have been removed from my mailing list and will receive no further emails from me.' One sentence. No qualifications. No 'removal takes 7-10 days'. Then: preference alternative (low-pressure, unsubscribe already stands). Then: graceful acknowledgment + re-subscribe link.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Subscriber removed — address suppressed immediately, door left open

name: Unsubscribe — Author's Choice
description: Fires at conclusion of failed re-engagement sequence. Three required elements: (1) clear statement of removal, (2) brief non-blaming explanation, (3) re-subscribe path. Tone: respectful, not apologetic. Transparency is non-negotiable.
goalExit: Subscriber removed transparently — re-subscribe path provided, compliance documented

steps.label: Trigger
steps.detail: Re-engagement sequence exhausted — subscriber remained unresponsive through all emails

steps.label: Removal Notification
steps.detail: Subject: 'I have removed you from my list' or 'You have been removed due to inactivity' or 'This is the last email I will send you' — (1) 'I have removed your address from my mailing list due to inactivity.' (2) Brief non-blaming explanation of why. (3) 'If you would like to stay connected, you can re-subscribe using the link below. I would genuinely be glad to have you back.'

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Subscriber removed — address moved to suppression list, removal documented for compliance

name: Subscription Renewal Reminder
description: Alerts subscribers before their plan renews — stating the renewal date, amount, and a clear management link. Framed as a service to the reader. For annual plans, sends a second reminder 3 days out.
goalExit: Subscription renewed — no further reminders sent

steps.label: Billing Trigger
steps.detail: Renewal date approaching — 7 days out (webhook from payment processor)

steps.label: Renewal Reminder (7 days)
steps.detail: Clearly state renewal date, amount, and how to manage subscription. Treat reader as partner in transaction. Briefly remind of value received.

steps.label: Wait 4 Days
steps.detail: Delay: 4 days

steps.label: Already Renewed?
steps.detail: If renewed — exit; else — send final reminder for annual plans

steps.label: Final Reminder (3 days out)
steps.detail: For annual subscriptions where higher charge warrants a second reminder.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Subscription renewed successfully

name: Payment Failed — Dunning Sequence
description: Three-email dunning sequence for failed recurring charges. Assumes good faith throughout — most failures are a billing system problem, not a reader problem. Access maintained during dunning window.
goalExit: Payment updated and subscription restored automatically

steps.label: Billing Trigger
steps.detail: Recurring charge fails to process (webhook: payment_failed)

steps.label: Email 1 — Immediate
steps.detail: Warm, blame-free. Access continues. Direct payment update link. Most failures are unintentional — usually an expired card.

steps.label: Wait 4 Days
steps.detail: Delay: 4 days — reader retains full access

steps.label: Payment Updated?
steps.detail: If updated — subscription restored, exit; else — Email 2

steps.label: Email 2 — Follow-up
steps.detail: Slightly more direct about timeline. Repeats fix link. Invites reply if having trouble.

steps.label: Wait 5 Days
steps.detail: Delay: 5 days

steps.label: Payment Updated?
steps.detail: If updated — exit; else — Email 3

steps.label: Email 3 — Final Notice
steps.detail: States deadline clearly. Genuine note that you would hate to lose them. After this, cancellation follows automatically.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Payment resolved — subscription restored. If unresolved, Author Choice End Subscription fires.

name: Upgrade / Downgrade Confirmation
description: Fires when a reader changes their subscription tier. Answers four questions immediately: what changed, when it takes effect, new billing amount, and what access changed.
goalExit: Plan change confirmed and reader understands new access

steps.label: Billing Trigger
steps.detail: Subscriber changes plan tier (webhook: subscription_updated)

steps.label: Upgrade or Downgrade?
steps.detail: Branch: upgrade — enthusiastic confirmation; downgrade — graceful confirmation, no win-back pitch

steps.label: Upgrade Confirmation
steps.detail: Genuinely warm. Confirms plan, effective date, new billing amount, new access.

steps.label: Downgrade Confirmation
steps.detail: Clear and respectful. No win-back pitch. Brief low-friction path to return if they change their mind.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Plan change confirmed — reader understands exactly what changed and when

name: End Subscription — Reader's Choice
description: Sent when a paying subscriber cancels. Part commercial documentation, part parting letter. Confirms cancellation clearly first — no retention campaign disguised as a confirmation.
goalExit: Cancellation confirmed, relationship preserved, door left open

steps.label: Billing Trigger
steps.detail: Subscriber cancels subscription (webhook: subscription_cancelled — reader-initiated)

steps.label: Cancellation Confirmation
steps.detail: First: plan cancelled, access end date, no further charges. Three sentences. Then: genuine acknowledgment of relationship. Then: low-pressure re-subscribe link.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Cancellation confirmed — reader exits paid relationship on positive terms, remains on free list

name: End Subscription — Author's Choice
description: Sent when the author ends a subscription — due to unresolved payment failure, terms violation, or product retirement. Tone varies by reason. Transparency and respect non-negotiable.
goalExit: Subscription ended cleanly — reader informed, refund referenced if applicable

steps.label: Billing Trigger
steps.detail: Author-initiated cancellation (webhook: subscription_cancelled — author-initiated, or dunning exhausted)

steps.label: Reason for Cancellation?
steps.detail: Branch: payment failure — matter-of-fact + re-subscribe link; product retirement — personal explanation + refund info

steps.label: End Sub — Payment Failure
steps.detail: Warm, blame-free. Explain subscription cancelled because payment could not be processed. Clear path to re-subscribe. Refund info if applicable.

steps.label: End Sub — Product Retirement
steps.detail: Honest explanation of retirement reason. Acknowledge inconvenience. State access end date, prorated refund amount and timeline. Personal, not a form letter.

steps.label: Goal Exit
steps.detail: Subscription ended — reader informed with clarity and care, goodwill preserved
